rulings.Case Title: PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. WHITE SNOW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Deem Admitted Req. For Admissions, Set One, To White Snow Corporation And Req. For Sanctions Of $810.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Admitted Request For Admissions, Set One, To White Snow Corporation And Request For Sanctions Of $810.00 Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Deem Admitted Requests for Admissions dated November 8, 2013 is unopposed and granted. Sanctions are awarded against defendant, White Snow Corp,. in the sum of $810.00 pursuant to CCP 2023.010 and shall be paid within thirty days of service of the Order herein. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA VILLANUEVA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GUARDIAN AD et al VS. SOUTHERN INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jay Trisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 16, Defendant Jay Trisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication. Defendant Trisko's motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiffs raise a triable issue of fact as to whether A and F Engineering, Inc. is a continuation of AJ Pacheco and whether Defendant Trisko's duty of care continued to A and F Engineering, Inc. as a result. (See Plaintiff's Undisputed Facts 21-23 and Pacheco declaration 5.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to Evidence Code § 452(d). Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to C.R.C. 3.1350(e)(4) and Evidence Code § 452(d)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA VILLANUEVA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GUARDIAN AD et al VS. SOUTHERN INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant James E. Mcgovern, Inc'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudcation As To Southern Insurance Company And First Comp Insurance Agency, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 16, Cross Defendant James E. Mcgovern, Inc'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudcation As To Southern Insurance Company And First Comp Insurance Agency, Inc. Defendant McGovern's motion for summary judgment as to Cross-Complainants Southern Insurance Company and FirstComp Insurance Agency is denied. Defendant fails to establish that the Cross-Complainants' reliance on the 2007 Workers' Compensation policy application was unjustified as a matter of law. (See Defendant's Undisputed Facts 32, 34-39.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to Evidence Code § 452(d). rulings.Case Title: ANDREA VILLANUEVA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GUARDIAN AD et al VS. SOUTHERN INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant James E. Mcgovern, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 16, Defendant James E. Mcgovern, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiff'S Complaint. Defendant McGovern's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant cannot establish, as a matter of law, that the Plaintiff fails to establish the damages element in both the negligence and breach of contract causes of action based on Valentine v. Membrila Insurance Services, Inc. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 462. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to Evidence Code § 452(d). Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted pursuant to C.R.C. 3.1350(e)(4) and Evidence Code § 452(d)(1). rulings.Case Title: IN RE: 66 CLEARY COURT CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 3, PETITIONER 66 CLEARY COURT CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION's Hearing On Petition. The petition is granted and complies with Civil Code sec. 4275(c). Respondent fails to demonstrate that petitioner engaged in advocacy in contravention of Civil Code sec. 5105. The amendment to the pet policy was not substantive because the original pet policy was still subject to the Board's pet rules. The amendment regarding rentals was reasonable. rulings.Case Title: LUIS ADOLFO MARQUEZ et al VS. PG & E et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moton For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTs INDUSTRIAL CREW, LLC and GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Denied without prejudice. Defendants do not present sufficient evidence establishing that the Tech-Bilt factors are met here. Conclusory allegations as to the settling party's liability are insufficient. (Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 834.) Further, substantial evidence showing the nature and extent of the settling defendant's liability is necessary and required; without such evidence, a determination of good faith is an abuse of discretion. (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arhur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JON BENNER VS. R.C. CHRONICLE BUILDING, L.P., R.C.C. (GP) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Further Discovery Responses And The Production Of Documents; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF JON BENNER'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And The Production Of Documents; Proof Of Service Off calendar as to Department 302. On August 21, 2013, the Court appointed a judicial referee, Hon. Edward Panelli (Ret.). to "decide all issues of fact and law submitted by the parties for decision in the same manner as required for a bench trial, including all law and motion matters, ex parte matters, and discovery disputes." (Order, issued Aug. 21, 2013, p. 3:10-12.) On December 2, 2013, pursuant to the stipulation of the parties, the Court consolidated Wanless v. R.C. Chronicle Building LP (case no. 534870) with Benner v. R.C. Chronicle Building LP (527401) and Gani v. R.C. Chronicle Building LP (case no. 534870) for all purposes, and the Court appointed Justice Panelli as judicial referee of the consolidated actions. (Order, issued Dec. 2, 2013, p. 4:15-21.) Moving party should re-set this motion before Justice Panelli. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: INVESTMENT RETRIEVERS, INC. VS. AFSANEH VALIZADEH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Deft
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF INVESTMENT RETRIEVERS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied as to defendants AFI and Associates, Inc. and Mr. Afsaneh Valizadeh. The Court overrules defendants' objections to the declarations of Ms. Sooth and Mr. Griesman. They meet the requirements of Evidence Code sec. 1271. Nevertheless, plaintiff fails to maintain its initial burden of production because the attached loan application and guarantee are illegible. Lastly, the declaration of Mr. Afsaneh Valizadeh creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether he executed the contract that plaintiff relies upon. rulings.Case Title: LILY OW et al VS. DITO, JOHN JOSEPH, ALSO KNOWN AS "JOHN J. DITO" et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Fmtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To Req. For Production Of Documents And Things (Set One), And Form Interrogatories (Set One), For Production Of Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS LILY OW, PEGGY OW'S Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Things (Set One), And Form Interrogatories (Set One), For Production Of Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is granted. It was the obligation of defendant, having requested an extension of time, to confirm said extension in writing. See CCP 2030.270(b) and 2031.270(b). Once the dispute over which extension date was operative became known and the parties could not resolve the issue through the "meet and confer" process, the statutory scheme to be relieved of a waiver of objections pursuant CCP 2030.290(a) and 2031.300(a)by the filing of motion became available. It was not followed. The defendant, simultaneously with his filing of its Opposition herein, on February 27, 2014, served further formal responses. The fact of the late service does not moot consideration of the issues raised by this motion. See Younger on California Motions (2011) para 29:56. Because the Court finds the initial service of responses on December 19, 2013 was untimely, no ruling on the adequacy of defendant' further interrogatory responses and production is necessary. Both discovery pleadings shall be responded to, without objection, within thirty days from the date of service of this Order. Sanctions are awarded against defense counsel only the sum of $2,500.00 to be paid within thirty days of service of this Order. The awarding of sanctions takes into account the fact plaintiff's counsel offered to provide additional time for the defendant to respond to the discovery and the offer was not accepted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EURLYN P. MAURICIO VS. ANA HERNANDEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Joinder In Mtn To Declare Pltf A Vexatious Litigant; Provide Security; Dismiss Action; And Prohibit Pltf From Filing New Lawsuits W/O Court Permission
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTs ANA HERNANDEZ, ABRAHAM BITAR and WALGREEN CO.'s Joinder In Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant; Provide Security; Dismiss Action; And Prohibit Plaintiff From Filing New Lawsuits without Court Permission. The motion is granted. On January 31, 2014 and February 27, 2014, the Court ordered plaintiff to provide a courtesy copy of his opposition papers. The Court warned plaintiff that the failure to comply with its order may result in the motion being granted. Plaintiff has failed to provide the courtesy copies. Therefore, the motion is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAL200 et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion In Support Of Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, March 12, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs FREMONT UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, SAN RAFAEL CITY SCHOOLS, WALNUT CREEK SCHOOL DISTRICT, WEST CONTRA COSTA UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT and ALAMEDA CITY UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Motion To Transfer Venue. The motion to change venue is denied. CCP 394 does not apply to this mandamus action seeking equitable/injunctive relief. Venue is proper under CCP 395 because one of the defendants resides in San Francisco. The motion to change venue based on the convenience of witnesses is premature because there are no answers on file. See Easton v. Superior Crt., (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 245; Buran Equipment Co., Inc. v. Sup Crt., (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 1662, 1665. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
